Record Class ProcessorInfo

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
io.joshuasalcedo.os.domain.hardware.ProcessorInfo
All Implemented Interfaces:
OSHardwareObject
public record ProcessorInfo(String name, Manufacturer manufacturer, String identifier, String processorId, String microarchitecture, int physicalPackages, int physicalCores, int logicalProcessors, long vendorFrequency, long maxFrequency, List<ProcessorInfo.CacheInfo> caches, List<ProcessorInfo.CoreTopology> topology) extends Record implements OSHardwareObject
Value object representing CPU/Processor information. Immutable snapshot of processor state from OSHI.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static final record 
    ProcessorInfo.CacheInfo
    Value object for processor cache information.
    static final record 
    ProcessorInfo.CoreTopology
    Value object for individual core topology.

    isBoostCapable()
    True if the max frequency is higher than the vendor base frequency (boost/turbo capable).
    boolean
    isHyperThreaded()
    True if this processor reports more logical processors than physical cores (HyperThreading / SMT).
    boolean
    isNuma()
    True if this processor has more than one NUMA node.

  • Constructor Details

    • ProcessorInfo

      public ProcessorInfo(String name, Manufacturer manufacturer, String identifier, String processorId, String microarchitecture, int physicalPackages, int physicalCores, int logicalProcessors, long vendorFrequency, long maxFrequency, List<ProcessorInfo.CacheInfo> caches, List<ProcessorInfo.CoreTopology> topology)
      Creates an instance of a ProcessorInfo record class.
      Parameters:
      name - the value for the name record component
      manufacturer - the value for the manufacturer record component
      identifier - the value for the identifier record component
      processorId - the value for the processorId record component
      microarchitecture - the value for the microarchitecture record component
      physicalPackages - the value for the physicalPackages record component
      physicalCores - the value for the physicalCores record component
      logicalProcessors - the value for the logicalProcessors record component
      vendorFrequency - the value for the vendorFrequency record component
      maxFrequency - the value for the maxFrequency record component
      caches - the value for the caches record component
      topology - the value for the topology record component

    • vendorFrequencyFormatted

      public String vendorFrequencyFormatted()

    • maxFrequencyFormatted

      public String maxFrequencyFormatted()

    • isBoostCapable

      public boolean isBoostCapable()
      True if the max frequency is higher than the vendor base frequency (boost/turbo capable).

    • isHyperThreaded

      public boolean isHyperThreaded()
      True if this processor reports more logical processors than physical cores (HyperThreading / SMT).

    • cachesAtLevel

      public List<ProcessorInfo.CacheInfo> cachesAtLevel(int level)
      All caches at the given level (1, 2, or 3).

    • numaNodes

      public List<Integer> numaNodes()
      All NUMA nodes present on this processor.

    • isNuma

      public boolean isNuma()
      True if this processor has more than one NUMA node.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. Reference components are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object); primitive components are compared with the compare method from their corresponding wrapper classes.
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.
